methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $46M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Hypersonix Launch Systems is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $46M AUD to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $46M total raised as a base proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $92M. The peer-median method anchors to three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), producing a median valuation of $300M with a range of $150M to $500M. The divergence between methods reflects uncertainty in peer selection and applicability of stage-based progression to early-stage defense tech.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $78M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $196M. This $267M spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Hypersonix more closely resembles lower-capitalized Series A peers or the higher-end of the defense tech comp set. Key sensitivities include technology maturity, customer traction, and defensibility relative to the sampled peer set, none of which are quantified in available data.
